channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trouble getting weight loss drugs covered by insurance? Here's what to know
item.description: If your doctor prescribes a GLP-1 medication for weight loss but your insurance won't cover it, you have options.
item.content:encoded:
If your doctor prescribes a GLP-1 medication for weight loss but your insurance won't cover it, you have options.

(Image credit: Oona Zenda)
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: Trump's Forest Service says it's fully staffed as fires erupt in the West
item.description: The Forest Service says it's fully staffed with seasonal firefighters going into summer but there are still questions about whether the government is prepared if major wildfires get out of hand.
item.content:encoded: The Forest Service says it's fully staffed with seasonal firefighters going into summer but there are still questions about whether the government is prepared if major wildfires get out of hand.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: The new mandatory hydration breaks at the World Cup continue to cause frustration
item.description: Several new soccer rules are being premiered during this World Cup. One of them is causing a lot of controversy: hydration breaks. Are they a good measure or a money-grabbing scheme?
item.content:encoded: Several new soccer rules are being premiered during this World Cup. One of them is causing a lot of controversy: hydration breaks. Are they a good measure or a money-grabbing scheme?
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: A college student found safety in his housemate in a moment of crisis
item.description: While in his first year at college, Stephen Parker turned to his housemate while in the throws of a panic attack. The care he received stuck with him for years.
item.content:encoded: While in his first year at college, Stephen Parker turned to his housemate while in the throws of a panic attack. The care he received stuck with him for years.

item.title: 50 years on, an EPA time capsule at the Kennedy Space Center remains a mystery
item.description: For the Bicentennial in 1976, the Environmental Protection Agency buried a time capsule with the promise to open it this year. It's a promise the agency may not fulfill.
item.content:encoded: For the Bicentennial in 1976, the Environmental Protection Agency buried a time capsule with the promise to open it this year. It's a promise the agency may not fulfill.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Turner

item.title: Greenspan's Fed legacy was tarnished when millions lost homes during financial crisis
item.description: Longtime Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan died Monday at age 100. He's remembered as a master of monetary policy, but his light touch on regulation also set the stage for a financial crisis.
item.content:encoded: Longtime Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan died Monday at age 100. He's remembered as a master of monetary policy, but his light touch on regulation also set the stage for a financial crisis.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: With Starmer's resignation, the U.K. is set for its 7th PM in 10 years
item.description: Andy Burnham, a newly elected member of Parliament, plans to run for prime minister, replacing Keir Starmer, who resigned Monday.
item.content:encoded: Andy Burnham, a newly elected member of Parliament, plans to run for prime minister, replacing Keir Starmer, who resigned Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Music lovers in Paris turned out in droves for the Fête de la Musique despite hot temps
item.description: Almost 50 years on, an American's idea for a citywide outdoor celebration of music in Paris has grown into Fête de la Musique, where the city's streets become a global music block party.
item.content:encoded: Almost 50 years on, an American's idea for a citywide outdoor celebration of music in Paris has grown into Fête de la Musique, where the city's streets become a global music block party.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Rosman

item.title: A look at the science that's been lost on long COVID
item.description: Since President Trump took office, hundreds of experts advising on science and research issues have been dismissed. One committee was on long COVID, an issue that doctors are desperate for help with.
item.content:encoded: Since President Trump took office, hundreds of experts advising on science and research issues have been dismissed. One committee was on long COVID, an issue that doctors are desperate for help with.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: 'Soaked into the wood': A visit to the birthplace of religious liberty
item.description: Religious liberty was one of the founding pursuits of America. Rhode Island was founded on the idea that the intermingling of religion and government damages both church and state.
item.content:encoded: Religious liberty was one of the founding pursuits of America. Rhode Island was founded on the idea that the intermingling of religion and government damages both church and state.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose
